recalls.Description: Evajoy has announced a recall for all above-ground pools measuring 48 inches and taller, including models EJ-HF032, EJ-HF044, and EJ-HF045. The EJ-HF032 model is 16 feet in diameter and 48 inches high, the EJ-HF044 model is 18 feet in diameter and 52 inches high, and the EJ-HF045 model is 24 feet in diameter and 52 inches high. Each pool features the Evajoy logo on the outer liner. The issue lies with a compression strap that encircles the pool's vertical support poles, potentially allowing children to climb into the pool and risk drowning, even when the ladder is removed. For pool safety resources, visit www.PoolSafely.gov for free educational materials on barriers, covers, and alarms.
recalls.Title: Evajoy Above-Ground Pools 48 Inches and Taller Recalled Due to Drowning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Evajoy at 888-846-2988 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email support@evajoy.co, or visit https://evajoy.co/pages/poolrecall. You can also find more information by going to www.evajoy.co and clicking on 'Product Recalls' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Evajoy Above-Ground Pool Model EJ-HF032
Recalled Evajoy Above-Ground Pool Model EJ-HF044
Recalled Evajoy Above-Ground Pool Model EJ-HF045

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Danya Tech Co., Ltd. d/b/a Evajoy, of China

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from March 2023 through June 2024, priced between $550 and $1,050 based on size and model.

Hazards.Name: Compression strap may create foothold, posing drowning risk to children.

Remedies.Name: Request a free repair kit from Evajoy to remove the compression strap while keeping the pool structurally sound. In the meantime, ensure children cannot access the pool unsupervised or drain the pool until the repair is completed.

recalls.metaDescription: Evajoy recalls above-ground pools 48 inches and taller due to drowning hazards. Learn how to ensure safety and request a repair kit.

recalls.Description: Watkins Manufacturing is recalling Hydromassage rotary jets (6 fin) used in Highlife Collection spas, including replacement parts. The affected models are Grandee, Envoy, Vanguard, Aria, Prodigy, Sovereign, Jetsetter, and Jetsetter LX from model years 2023, 2024, and 2025. These jets can pose serious risks of entanglement and drowning due to their suction capabilities.
recalls.Title: Watkins Manufacturing Issues Recall for Hydromassage Rotary Jets in Highlife Collection Spas Due to Drowning and Entanglement Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled jets immediately and visit http://www.hotspring.com/recalls to check if their spa is affected. For further assistance, contact Watkins Wellness at 888-450-5748 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email jetrecall@watkinsmfg.com.

Products.Name: Hydromassage Rotary Jet (6 fin) in Highlife Collection spas
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 32,900 units (In addition, about 853 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Highlife Spa Collection
Location of the serial number on the Highlife Collection spas
Recalled Hydromassage rotary jet with 6 fins
The recalled jets have a red circle around them

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a consumer whose hair was entangled in the jets. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through independent pool and spa dealers across the U.S. from October 2022 to September 2025, priced between $16,000 and $24,000 for spas and $18 to $35 for replacement parts.

Importers.Name: Jet Importer: Watkins Manufacturing Corporation, of Vista, California

Hazards.Name: Entanglement and drowning hazards due to suction force of the jets

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the jets and check the firm's website. If affected, they will receive instructions and a video for safely turning off the jets and installing free replacement inserts.

recalls.metaDescription: Watkins recalls Hydromassage rotary jets in Highlife Collection spas due to drowning risks. Check if your spa is affected and learn how to remedy the issue.

recalls.Description:
Starmatrix Group has issued a recall for all Sunneday and Blue Bay brand above-ground pools that are 48 inches or taller. These pools feature a compression strap around the vertical support poles, which can create a foothold that allows children to access the pool, leading to a potential drowning hazard. The model information and brand logos are printed on the pool liner's exterior. Consumers should be aware of the risks associated with these pools, even when ladders are removed.

The affected pool models include various sizes, such as the Sunneday Oasis Cottage and Blue Bay Softsided Pools. To prioritize safety, it is crucial for parents and guardians to ensure that children cannot access the pools unsupervised. For immediate safety, consider draining the pool until the necessary repairs can be made.
recalls.Title: Starmatrix Group Recalls Sunneday and Blue Bay Above-Ground Pools Due to Drowning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers can reach Starmatrix at their toll-free number (888) 254-2714 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additionally, inquiries can be sent via email to info@starmatrix.cn or through their website at https://www.starmatrix.cn/page-81484.html. For more details, visit www.starmatrix.cn and click on 'Product Recalls' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Sunneday Oasis Cottage Above-Ground Pool Models 70018 and 70024
Recalled Blue Bay Above-Ground Pool Models 358015W, 359015G, 360018W, 361018G, and 380018T

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com and www.sunneday.com from January 2024 through October 2025, priced between $500 and $1,210 based on size and model.

Hazards.Name: Compression strap may create foothold, posing drowning risk to children

Remedies.Name: Request a free repair kit from Starmatrix; ensure children cannot access the pool unattended or drain the pool until repairs are made.

recalls.metaDescription: Starmatrix recalls Sunneday and Blue Bay above-ground pools due to drowning hazards. Learn how to ensure safety and request repairs.

recalls.Description: Blue Wave is recalling all above-ground pools measuring 48 inches and taller due to a potential drowning hazard. The model details can be found on the original packaging and instruction manual, with the Blue Wave logo displayed on the pool liner. The affected models include various sizes and styles, such as the Blue Wave 15-Ft Round 48-In Active Frame Pool and the Blue Wave 24-Ft Round 52-In Dark Cocoa Wicker Frame Pool, among others. Each model has a unique UPC code for identification.
recalls.Title: Blue Wave Recalls Above-Ground Pools 48 Inches and Taller Due to Drowning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Blue Wave at their toll-free number (800) 603-0475, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, inquiries can be sent via email to recall@bluewaveproducts.com or through their website at www.bluewaveproducts.com/pages/recall. For more information, visit www.bluewaveproducts.com and click on 'Product Recalls' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Blue Wave 15-Ft Round 48-In Active Frame Pool

Retailers.Name: Available at The Home Depot, Lowe's, and Dunham's Sports stores nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and Wayfair.com from January 2021 through July 2025, priced between $600 and $2,000 based on size and model.

Importers.Name: Blue Wave Products Inc., of Batavia, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Compression strap may create a foothold, allowing child access to pool, posing drowning risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should contact Blue Wave for a free repair kit. In the meantime, ensure children cannot access the pool unattended or drain the pool until repairs are made.

recalls.metaDescription: Blue Wave recalls above-ground pools 48 inches and taller due to drowning risk. Contact for free repair kit and safety information.

recalls.Description: SereneLife is recalling all above-ground pools measuring 48 inches or taller, including models SLPORND15 and SLPORND18. These pools feature a compression strap that wraps around the vertical support poles, potentially allowing children to climb into the pool and face a drowning hazard. Even with the ladder removed, children can still access the pools due to these footholds. For safety resources, visit CPSC's website at www.PoolSafely.gov for free educational materials on pool safety, including barriers and alarms.
recalls.Title: SereneLife Recalls Above-Ground Pools 48 Inches and Taller Due to Drowning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact SereneLife Home at 888-619-6770 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@serenelifehome.com, or visit https://serenelifehome.com/pages/recalls. You can also find more information by going to https://serenelifehome.com/ and clicking on 'Product Recalls' on the homepage.

Products.Name: SereneLife Above-Ground Pools 48 Inches and Taller

Images.Caption: Recalled SereneLife 48” Above-Ground Pool Models SLPORND15 and SLPORND1

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Wayfair.com, Target.com, and the SereneLife website (serenelifehome.com) from December 2021 through May 2025, priced between $300 and $500 based on size and model.

Importers.Name: Sound Around Inc. dba SereneLife Home

Hazards.Name: Drowning risk due to foothold created by compression strap around pool legs.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the recalled pools and contact SereneLife Home for a full refund, providing proof of destruction or disposal.

recalls.metaDescription: SereneLife recalls 48-inch and taller above-ground pools due to drowning hazards. Contact them for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: TopHomer has issued a recall for 8-inch pool drain covers due to potential entrapment and drowning hazards, violating the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act. These drain covers are white, made of ABS plastic, with a top grate measuring 7.2 inches in diameter and a bottom mounting plate of 8 inches. Key markings include "Listed suction fitting," "SP-1053-B," and "88D9 148 GPM" on the grate's center, along with instructions for securing the grate on the outer ring.
recalls.Title: TopHomer Pool Drain Covers Recalled for Serious Entrapment and Drowning Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact TopHomerUS via email at Tophomer.recall@outlook.com or visit the TopHomerUS Amazon Seller Profile page for assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled TopHomer Pool Drain Cover
Recalled TopHomer Pool Drain Cover and Base

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from August 2024 to October 2025, priced between $15 and $26.

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and drowning hazards due to non-compliance with safety standards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled drain covers immediately, remove them from the pool, photograph the removed cover, and email the image to Tophomer.recall@outlook.com for a refund. Ensure all pools have VGBA-compliant covers and educate children about drain safety.

recalls.metaDescription: TopHomer recalls pool drain covers due to serious entrapment and drowning risks. Learn how to get a refund and ensure pool safety.

recalls.Description: Werner Paddles is recalling its Stealth and Covert kayak paddles due to a serious safety concern. These paddles, made from black carbon fiber, feature the Werner brand name on decals located on both blades, with the model name printed on the right paddle blade. The paddles may break during use, which poses a drowning hazard to users.
recalls.Title: Werner Paddles Issues Recall for Stealth and Covert Kayak Paddles Due to Drowning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Werner Paddles at 800-275-3311 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, email stealthcovert@wernerpaddles.com, or visit https://wernerpaddles.com/pages/covert-stealth-recall-consumers for more details.

Products.Name: Stealth Kayak Paddle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 700 (In addition, about 25 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Werner Stealth Kayak Paddle
Location of Model Name
Recalled Werner Covert Kayak Paddle
Location of Model Name

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 80 reports of the paddles breaking during use. No injuries have been reported.

Hazards.Name: Risk of drowning due to paddle breakage

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled paddles and contact Werner Paddles for a full refund or store credit. Complete the recall form at https://wernerpaddles.com/pages/covert-stealth-recall-consumers. To receive the refund or credit, destroy the paddle by cutting it into four equal pieces and upload a photo of the destroyed paddle.

recalls.metaDescription: Werner Paddles recalls Stealth and Covert kayak paddles over drowning risks. Contact Werner for refunds and safety instructions.

recalls.Description:
Certain above-ground pools from Bestway, Intex, and Polygroup, measuring 48 inches and taller, are being recalled due to a significant drowning hazard. The affected models include Bestway and Coleman pools sold from 2008 to 2024, Intex pools available from 2002 to 2012, and various Polygroup models sold from 2006 to 2025. Specific models can be identified by the brand names and model numbers printed on the pool liner. 
 Consumers are urged to take immediate action as the compression strap around the pool legs can create a foothold, potentially allowing children to access the pool unsupervised. To ensure safety, it is recommended to keep children away from the pool or drain it until repairs are made.
recalls.Title: Recall of Bestway, Intex, and Polygroup Above-Ground Pools 48 Inches and Taller Due to Drowning Risk; Nine Fatalities Reported
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Bestway at 844-593-4003 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://BWrecallsupport.expertinquiry.com or email BWrecallsupport5516@sedgwick.com. Intex can be reached at 800-549-8829 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or online at www.Intexcorp.com/recall or email recallhelp@intexcorp.com. For Polygroup, call 888-621-4137 from 5 a.m. to 11 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://polygrouprecall.com or email poolrecall@polygroup.com.

Products.Name: Above-Ground Pools 48 Inches and Taller
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 5 million (In addition, approximately 266,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Example of child using the compression strap to stand on above-ground pool, illustrating the hazard involved
Examples of recalled above-ground pools with arrows showing the compression strap running externally on the outside and over the vertical support legs
Examples of Strap Configurations for Recalled Above-Ground Pools 48-Inches and Taller
Examples of Strap Configurations Running Behind the Pole for Above-Ground Pools 48-Inches and Taller that are Not Recalled

Injuries.Name: CPSC believes that nine children between the ages of 22 months and 3 years old have drowned, after gaining access to the pools via the footholds. The incidents occurred in California, Texas, Florida, Michigan, Wisconsin and Missouri between 2007 and 2022. CPSC is also aware of three other incidents in 2011 and 2012 where children who gained access to the recalled pools were reported to have previously used the compression strap to gain entry to the pool.

Retailers.Name: Available at various retailers nationwide, including Walmart, Target, Sears, Lowe's, Kmart, Toys 'R' Us, Sam's Club, The Home Depot, Big Lots, Costco, BJ's, and online at Amazon.com, Wayfair.com, and brand-specific websites.

Distributors.Name: Polygroup North America Inc. (El Paso, Texas)

Hazards.Name: Drowning Risk Due to Compression Strap Design

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert for Bestway, Intex, and Polygroup above-ground pools due to drowning hazards. Contact manufacturers for repair kits.

recalls.Description: Jeriflyer is recalling its 8-inch pool drain covers due to a serious risk of entrapment, which could lead to severe injury or death. These white plastic drain covers, measuring 8.1 inches in diameter, do not meet the safety requirements set forth by the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act. This poses a significant danger to swimmers and bathers.
recalls.Title: Jeriflyer Pool Drain Covers Recalled for Entrapment Hazard Violating Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Jeriflyer via email at Junjuanshop2025@outlook.com, or visit Amazon.com: Jerliflyer and select 'Recall' at the top of the page for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Jeriflyer pool drain cover
Recalled Jeriflyer pool drain cover
Recalled Jeriflyer pool drain cover

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from April 2024 to May 2025, priced between $10 and $15.

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to non-compliance with safety standards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled drain covers immediately. To obtain a refund, consumers should remove the cover, photograph it, and email the image to Junjuanshop2025@outlook.com. Ensure that all pools have VGBA compliant drain covers and educate children about staying away from drains. Junjuanshop and Amazon will directly contact all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Jeriflyer recalls 8-inch pool drain covers due to serious entrapment hazards. Learn how to get a refund and ensure safety compliance.

recalls.Description: Endless Pools is recalling its Manual Retractable Security Pool Covers due to potential drowning and entrapment hazards. These covers feature a hand crank mechanism for easy operation and were available in various colors such as tan, black, gray, light blue, and royal blue. They are designed to fit custom Endless Pools ranging from 8 to 16 feet wide. Improper installation can create a dangerous gap, failing to meet safety standards and posing serious risks to children.
recalls.Title: Endless Pools Recalls Manual Retractable Pool Covers Due to Drowning and Entrapment Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Endless Pools at 800-910-2714 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at poolhelp@endlesspools.com. More information is available online at https://myendlesspool.com/s/support/product-recalls or by visiting https://myendlesspool.com/s/ and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Manual Retractable Security Pool Covers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 11,500 (In addition, about 480 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Manual Retractable Pool Security Cover in open position
Wall Mount Configuration
Below Deck Mount Configuration

Retailers.Name: Available through Endless Pools and authorized dealers across the country, as well as online at www.endlesspools.com, from January 1994 to December 2024, priced between $1,000 and $2,200.

Hazards.Name: Drowning and entrapment hazards due to improper installation causing gaps between the cover and pool surface.

Remedies.Name: Free inspection and repair services provided by Endless Pools to address entrapment and drowning risks.

recalls.metaDescription: Endless Pools recalls Manual Retractable Pool Covers due to drowning risks. Contact for free inspections and repairs to ensure safety.

recalls.Description: NICREW is recalling its Submersible RGB LED Lights, model number N21743, which come in a 15-inch black tube and include a black remote control featuring 21 buttons powered by a CR2032 lithium coin battery. The design poses a serious risk as the battery can be easily accessed by children, leading to potential ingestion hazards. This recall is in violation of federal regulations concerning consumer products with coin batteries, as the products lack necessary warnings.
recalls.Title: Recall of NICREW Submersible RGB LED Lights Due to Ingestion Risk from Coin Batteries
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers should reach out to Nicrew via email at nicrew@support.com or visit www.nicrew.com/n21743-recall/ or navigate to the Product Recalls section at the bottom of their website.

Products.Name: NICREW 15" Submersible RGB LED Lights with Remote Control

Images.Caption: Recalled Submersible RGB LED Light with Remote Control
Recalled Submersible RGB LED Light with Remote Control packaging

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.Amazon.com from May 2024 through August 2024 for approximately $17.

Distributors.Name: Nicrew Technology Co. Limited, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to accessible lithium coin battery in the remote control, violating federal safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product immediately, remove the battery, and contact Nicrew for a free remote replacement and disposal instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: NICREW recalls Submersible RGB LED Lights due to ingestion risks from coin batteries. Contact Nicrew for a free replacement and safety information.

recalls.Description: A recall has been issued for the Aiper Seagull Pro cordless robotic pool vacuum cleaner, model ZT6001. This vacuum weighs around 24 pounds and measures approximately 19.5 x 18.5 x 15 inches. The brand name "Aiper" is displayed on the front of the dark gray unit, while the model name and serial number, starting with S/N "61", can be found on a label underneath the vacuum.
recalls.Title: Aiper Seagull Pro Cordless Robotic Pool Vacuum Cleaners Recalled for Burn and Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the affected pool vacuums immediately and contact Aiper at 888-968-0666 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also reach out via email at recall@aiper.com or visit https://aiper.com/seagullpro-recall/ for further details.

Products.Name: Aiper Seagull Pro ZT6001 Cordless Robotic Pool Vacuum Cleaner
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 32,660 (In addition, about 2,530 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Aiper Seagull Pro Cordless Robotic Pool Cleaner
Label with model name and serial number located on the bottom of the recalled pool vacuum

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 19 reports of the recalled pool vacuums melting, smoking or catching on fire while charging causing five incidents of property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Dongguan Yuanhong Intelligent Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available at Best Buy, Home Depot, Lowe's, and Walmart stores nationwide, as well as online at www.aiper.com, Amazon.com, Bestbuy.com, HomeDepot.com, Lowes.com, and Walmart.com from March 2023 through May 2024, priced between $700 and $900.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Aiper Intelligent Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Overheating current adapter posing burn and fire hazards

Remedies.Name: Consumers will receive a free replacement Aiper Scuba S1 robotic pool cleaner after returning the recalled unit. Aiper will provide a return shipping label and will ship the replacement within 15 days of receiving the recalled product. Aiper is also reaching out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of Aiper Seagull Pro cordless robotic pool vacuum cleaners due to burn and fire hazards. Contact Aiper for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Sublue is recalling specific lithium-ion batteries used in their Mix underwater scooters due to a potential fire hazard. The affected batteries are exclusively black, while the scooters are available in arctic white, space blue, and aqua blue. Each scooter features the 'Sublue' logo on the top and 'Whiteshark Mix Underwater Scooter' on the side, with the battery compartment located underneath the device.
recalls.Title: Sublue Recalls Black Lithium-Ion Batteries for Mix Underwater Scooters Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Sublue at 855-206-8698 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, seven days a week, email support.us@sublue.com, or visit www.sublue.com/recalls for further details.

Products.Name: Sublue Mix Underwater Scooters and Black Replacement Batteries

Images.Caption: Recalled Sublue Mix Underwater Scooter in arctic white
Recalled Sublue Mix Underwater Scooter in aqua blue
Recalled Sublue Mix Underwater Scooter in space blue
Recalled Sublue Mix Underwater Scooter with “Whiteshark Mix” label
Recalled Sublue Mix battery

Injuries.Name: Sublue has received 161 reports of thermal and swelling incidents involving the lithium-ion batteries, including eight reports of fire, one report of injury, and two reports of property damage totaling $651,400.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.amazon.com, www.costco.com, www.sublue.com, www.westmarine.com, and other sites from June 2018 to February 2025, priced around $550 for scooters and $150 for replacement batteries.

Importers.Name: Sublue Technology Inc., of Walnut, California

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to potential overheating and ignition of lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Free replacement with a new generation gray battery; return of recalled battery required

recalls.metaDescription: Sublue recalls black lithium-ion batteries for Mix underwater scooters due to fire hazards. Contact Sublue for a safe battery replacement.

recalls.Description: GOETAS has issued a recall for its pool drain covers due to a serious entrapment hazard. These white plastic covers, designed for swimming pools, stand approximately 7 inches tall with a top grate diameter of about 9 inches. They do not meet the safety standards set by the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act, which could pose risks to swimmers and bathers.
recalls.Title: GOETAS Pool Drain Covers Recalled for Entrapment Risk; Noncompliance with Safety Act; Available Exclusively on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach out to GOETAS via email at Atlaspool@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled GOETAS Pool Drain Cover – with top grate
Recalled GOETAS Pool Drain Cover – without top grate
Recalled GOETAS Pool Drain Cover – side view
Recalled GOETAS Pool Drain Cover – front view

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from March 2022 through July 2024 for approximately $61.

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to noncompliance with safety regulations

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled drain covers immediately, remove and dispose of them. Email a photo of the cover to Atlaspool@163.com to obtain a full refund. Ensure compliance with VGBA standards for all pool drain covers and educate children about drain safety. Amazon is notifying all known buyers.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of GOETAS Pool Drain Covers due to entrapment risks. Immediate action required for safety compliance.

recalls.Description: Delta Cycle is recalling its Ceiling Hoists with Straps, model RS2300, which can lift heavy items like bicycles and kayaks using a pulley system. The model number is found on the product packaging. If the hoists are used without straps, no action is necessary. Affected units have serial numbers within specific ranges or may not have a serial number at all. The serial number can be located on the underside of the pulley where it attaches to the ceiling.
recalls.Title: Delta Cycle Recalls Ceiling Hoists with Straps Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Delta Cycle Corp at 800-474-6615 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email hoiststraprecall@deltacycle.com, or visit https://designbydelta.com/hoiststraprecall for further details.

Products.Name: Ceiling Hoist with Straps
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 25,140 (In addition, about 3,390 were sold in Canada)

Recalled product packaging showing Model RS2300

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of incidents, including one minor injury.

Retailers.Name: Available at REI, various independent retailers, Amazon.com, and designbydelta.com from February 2017 through July 2024 for approximately $40.

Importers.Name: Delta Cycle Corp., of Randolph, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury due to potential breakage of plastic buckles on straps

Remedies.Name: Stop using the Ceiling Hoist with Straps and contact Delta Cycle for free replacement straps. Cut and discard old straps.

recalls.metaDescription: Delta Cycle recalls Ceiling Hoists with Straps due to injury risks. Check for affected models and learn how to get replacement straps.

recalls.Description: TonGass is recalling its 8-inch round pool drain covers, specifically Model WGX1048E, due to non-compliance with the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act. These plastic drain covers, which feature two screws and measure 7-7/8 inches in diameter, pose a potential entrapment hazard for swimmers and bathers. The recall affects products sold exclusively on Amazon.com from September 2023 through June 2024.
recalls.Title: TonGass Pool Drain Covers Recalled for Safety Violations and Entrapment Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact TonGass via email at tongass@126.com, visit their Amazon Seller Profile, or call Amazon's toll-free number at 888-280-4331.

Images.Caption: Recalled TonGass pool drain cover (top view)
Recalled TonGass pool drain cover (bottom view)
Recalled TonGass pool drain cover (side view)

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com for $23 from September 2023 through June 2024.

Importers.Name: Oneida International Ltd., dba TonGass, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to non-compliance with VGBA standards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled drain covers immediately. To obtain a refund, remove the drain cover, take a photo, and email it to tongass@126.com. Ensure compliance with VGBA standards for all pool drain covers and educate children about drain safety. TonGass will reach out to all buyers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: TonGass recalls pool drain covers for safety violations, posing entrapment risks. Learn how to get a refund and ensure pool safety.

recalls.Description: TOPINCN is recalling its 1.5-inch and 3-inch round pool drain covers designed for swimming pools. The 1.5-inch cover features an outer diameter of approximately 4 inches and an inner diameter of about 2 inches, while the 3-inch cover measures around 5 inches in outer diameter and 3 inches in inner diameter. Both models are white and constructed from plastic, equipped with two screws.
recalls.Title: Recall of TOPINCN Pool Drain Covers Due to Entrapment Risk; Non-Compliance with Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach out to Sanure via email at sanure710@126.com or visit their Amazon Storefront page at https://www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&isAmazonFulfilled=1&marketplaceSeller=1&orderID=114-2704361-2776233&seller=A3FYRQNET3AI4U.

Recalled TOPINCN pool drain cover (bottom view)
Recalled TOPINCN pool drain cover (side view)

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at www.amazon.com from September 2022 to March 2024, priced between $9 and $32.

Importers.Name: Dong Guan Shi Li Peng Zhi Dian Zi Shang Wu You Xian Gong Si, dba Sanure Storefront, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to non-compliance with Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act (VGBA) standards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled drain covers immediately, remove them from the pool, photograph the covers, and email the images to sanure710@126.com to obtain a full refund. It's essential to ensure all pools comply with VGBA standards and educate children about staying clear of drains. Sanure will contact all buyers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: TOPINCN recalls pool drain covers due to entrapment hazards. Stop using them immediately and contact Sanure for a refund.
